Head tilted back, mouth open in haunting song, throat exposed to the fog-wrapped moon above turbulent waves.
Fingers dragging through moonlit water, creating silver ripples that spread outward in hypnotic concentric circles around her.
Bone comb pulling through wet tangles, seated on sea-slicked rock as strands fall across bare shoulders in dark ribbons.
Rising from a wave crest, arms lifted skyward, seawater cascading off her shoulders in sheets of white foam.
Reclining on barnacle-covered rocks, iridescent tail curled beneath her, one arm draped languidly over weathered stone.
Hand trailing through phosphorescent tide, leaving glowing blue-green streaks that illuminate her submerged scales.
Perched at a tidal pool's edge, leaning forward to study her reflection in the still, mirror-like surface.
Arching backward over a jagged rock, spine curved, dark hair trailing into churning surf below.
Examining a tarnished pocket watch, turning it in her fingers, surrounded by collected shells and sailor's trinkets.
Lips barely touching a conch shell's rim, whispering secrets into its spiral chamber, eyes half-closed.
Twisting to look over her shoulder, fingers combing through floating hair, moonlight catching on wet scales.
Reaching upward toward storm clouds, mouth open in a cry, waves crashing violently against her rocky perch.
Draped across driftwood, one arm extended toward the water, fingertips grazing the surface tension.
Pulling a drowned sailor's compass from the depths, holding it close to her face in dim twilight.
Suspended in a vertical dive, tail above water, arms stretched downward into dark green depths.
Pressing a seashell to her ear, head tilted, listening to ocean whispers with eyes closed in concentration.
Emerging from underwater cave darkness, parting curtains of seaweed with both outstretched arms.
Wringing out long wet hair, twisted into a rope, water streaming down her arched back.
Clinging to a ship's figurehead torn from the bow, cheek pressed against its wooden face.
Coiling around a submerged anchor chain, serpentine tail wrapped tight, pulling herself upward hand over hand.
Sinking beneath the surface, looking upward, one hand raised toward fading moonlight filtered through waves.
Balancing on a rocky outcrop's point, arms spread for equilibrium, tail extended behind for counterweight.
Clutching a waterlogged love letter, bringing the disintegrating paper close to read in failing light.
Leaning forward over a rock edge, hair falling like a curtain, peering into deep water below.
Throat extended in song, veins visible, fingers pressed against her own neck to feel the vibration.
